text: Structuring (also smurfing) describes the breakdown of large transactions into smaller amounts to avoid currency-transaction reporting thresholds. Indicators include rapid, repeated cash deposits below the reporting threshold across multiple branches or by multiple individuals depositing into a common account.

Layering through shell entities involves moving funds rapidly across legal vehicles that have no apparent commercial activity. Indicators include same-day onward wires, circular flows between related entities, and minimal economic purpose stated in payment messages.

Trade-based money laundering exploits the trade system through over- or under-invoicing, phantom shipments, and multiple invoicing. Indicators include invoice prices significantly above prevailing market levels for the goods described, or shipments with no corresponding goods movement.

Cross-border flows involving FATF black-list or grey-list jurisdictions warrant enhanced due diligence. Indicators include first-time corridors with no prior account history, payments routed through jurisdictions unrelated to the customer's stated business, and rapid pass-through accounts in high-risk jurisdictions.
